info.name: Sociotechnical Information Environment
info.description: The environment in which a transformer language model operates: human users producing prompts and consuming generated text, training corpora that shaped the model's parameters at training time, and physical compute infrastructure that supplies energy and absorbs heat during inference.

info.name: Users
info.description: Humans producing natural-language prompts. Each prompt is a request for text generation conditioned on the prompt's content.

info.name: Training Corpus
info.description: Frozen historical text data that shaped the model's parameters during training. After training, this corpus does not flow live into the model — its influence persists as the trained weights and the loss-derived behavioral constraints.

info.name: Compute Infrastructure
info.description: Physical hardware (GPUs, TPUs, CPUs) supplying electrical energy and FLOPS to the model during inference. Substitutable substrate — the model's specification is hardware-independent.

info.name: Users
info.description: Humans receiving generated text. Same external entity as Src-1.0 (linked via is_same_as_id) — users both produce prompts and consume outputs.

info.name: Heat Dissipation
info.description: Thermal waste from FLOP execution. Physical environment absorbing the thermodynamic cost of inference.

info.name: Transformer Language Model
info.description: An instance of the Generation-class instrument (per Halcyonic Systems' instruments catalog, §03). Computes: next-token probability conditioned on all prior tokens, compressing the statistical structure of human language into billions of parameters. Ceiling: knows what has been said about systems; does not know what systems are. Cannot make the ontological commitments that formal specification requires — does not know which claims to stake credibility on. First in a planned series of formal models for computational instruments (Recognition, Prediction, Generation, Reasoning, Decision, Discovery).

info.name: Model Specification Boundary
info.description: Encloses the trained parameters and computational graph. Does not include the runtime orchestration layer (batching, KV-cache, kernel scheduling) or the hardware substrate — those are environment. The boundary is precise (porosity 0: prompts and generated text cross only via defined interfaces) but semantically fuzzy (perceptive_fuzziness 0.5: the model's competence boundary across language, code, and concept domains is not crisply defined).

info.name: Prompt Intake
info.description: Receives natural-language prompts from users for tokenization.

info.name: Generated Text Output
info.description: Emits sampled, detokenized text back to users.

info.name: Training Influence
info.description: One-time historical inflow during training: gradients derived from the loss function over the training corpus. After training this interface is dormant — the corpus's influence persists only as baked-in weights and the Training Objective Constraint Force.

info.name: Energy Consumption
info.description: Imports electrical power from compute infrastructure to drive forward-pass FLOPS.

info.name: Heat Emission
info.description: Exports thermodynamic waste to the environment — the irreducible energy cost of computation.

systems.history: Trained once on a frozen corpus; weights fixed at deployment. Inference is stateless per request modulo the context window.
systems.transformation: text → tokens → vectors → contextualized vectors → logits → sampled token → text

info.name: Tokenizer
info.description: Segments raw text into discrete subword units (BPE, SentencePiece, or similar) and assigns each unit an integer ID from a fixed vocabulary. Reverses on output: detokenizes sampled IDs back to text. Boundary between continuous human language and the discrete symbolic units the rest of the model operates on.

info.name: Embedding Layer
info.description: Learned lookup mapping each token ID to a high-dimensional vector. Adds positional encoding so the transformer stack can distinguish token order. The output of this subsystem is a sequence of dense vectors — the geometric representation on which all subsequent computation operates.

info.name: Transformer Stack
info.description: Stack of N transformer layers, each combining multi-head self-attention with a position-wise feed-forward network, with residual connections and layer normalization. The computational core: refines token vectors into context-aware hidden states. Each layer attends across the full context window; depth produces increasingly abstract representations. This is the subsystem most directly constrained by the Training Objective Constraint Force.

info.name: Output Head
info.description: Linear projection from the transformer stack's final hidden state at the current position to a vector of size |vocabulary|, followed by softmax to produce a probability distribution over the next token. Does not sample — only constructs the distribution.

systems.transformation: hidden state → vocabulary probability distribution (logits + softmax)

info.name: Sampler
info.description: Samples a discrete token ID from the output head's probability distribution under a sampling policy (temperature, top-k, top-p / nucleus, beam search). Returns the sampled token to the Tokenizer for detokenization, and feeds it back into the input sequence for the next autoregressive step.

info.name: Trained Weights
info.description: The frozen parameter tensors that resulted from training. Architecturally, this is the model's knowledgebase — the encoded embodiment of the loss function applied to the training corpus. Mobus parallel: as DNA is the knowledgebase that constrains a cell's protein production, the trained weights are the knowledgebase that constrains the Transformer Stack's hidden-state production. The weights are read every forward pass but do not change during inference. Source of the Training Objective Constraint Force on C0.3.

systems.transformation: frozen parameters — read-only at inference; updated only during training

info.name: Prompt Receiver
info.description: Interface processor at I0.0 Prompt Intake. Receives raw natural-language prompts from the boundary and hands them to the Tokenizer for segmentation. Does not process semantics — just marshals the text from the outside world into the internal pipeline.

info.name: Output Marshaller
info.description: Interface processor at I0.1 Generated Text Output. Takes detokenized text from the Tokenizer and emits it to the boundary for delivery to the user. Does not generate content — just marshals it across the boundary.

info.name: Gradient Receiver
info.description: Interface processor at I0.2 Training Influence. During training, received loss gradients from the training corpus and routed them to the Trained Weights subsystem for parameter updates. Dormant after training — retained in the model to make the training-time boundary crossing explicit and the post-training constraint (F0.5 Force) traceable to its origin.

info.name: User Prompt
info.description: Natural-language text from a user, entering the model for tokenization and contextualization.

info.name: Generated Text
info.description: Detokenized natural-language output produced autoregressively, one token at a time.

info.name: Training Gradients
info.description: Historical (training-time only) flow of loss gradients from the training corpus into the model, shaping parameters via stochastic gradient descent. Dormant after training; included to make the historical influence explicit.

info.name: Electricity
info.description: Electrical power supplied by compute infrastructure to drive FLOP execution.

info.name: Heat
info.description: Thermodynamic waste — the irreducible energy cost of computation, dissipated to the environment.

info.name: Token Stream
info.description: Sequence of integer token IDs produced by the Tokenizer for embedding lookup.

info.name: Embedding Vectors
info.description: Sequence of dense vectors with positional encoding — the geometric representation entering the transformer stack.

info.name: Hidden States
info.description: Contextualized hidden state at the current generation position, output by the final transformer layer.

info.name: Vocabulary Distribution
info.description: Probability distribution over the vocabulary for the next token, ready for sampling.

info.name: Sampled Token
info.description: The discrete token ID drawn from the vocabulary distribution under the active sampling policy. Returned to the Tokenizer for detokenization to text.

info.name: Training Objective Constraint
info.description: The loss function (typically cross-entropy over next-token prediction) shaped the Trained Weights (C0.6) during training. Now, every inference, the weights persistently constrain the Transformer Stack's behavior — every output reflects the objective the model was trained to minimize. Per SL §1.7, this is a Force: influence without substance transfer. C0.6 holds the encoded constraint; C0.3 is the active computation that is constrained by it. Structurally analogous to bitcoin/ethereum/cosmos/solana's Protocol → Validating Force (Protocol subsystem holds consensus rules; Validating subsystem is constrained by them). Same SL pattern, different domain: a knowledgebase subsystem constraining a computational subsystem without material exchange.

info.name: Raw Prompt Text
info.description: Natural-language text marshalled from the boundary (via C0.7) to the Tokenizer for segmentation into token IDs.

info.name: Detokenized Output Text
info.description: Natural-language text produced by the Tokenizer (detokenization of sampled IDs) and handed to C0.8 for emission across the boundary.

info.name: Gradient Delivery
info.description: Training-time flow of loss gradients from the Gradient Receiver to the Trained Weights, updating parameters via stochastic gradient descent. Dormant after training.
